items.question: What does perplexity control in t-SNE?
options: The effective neighborhood size used when building high-D Gaussian affinities
The learning rate for the supervised loss
The number of PCA components retained
items.answer: The effective neighborhood size used when building high-D Gaussian affinities
items.explanation: Perplexity sets target entropy for p(j|i); σ_i is chosen per point to match that neighborhood scale.

items.question: Which kernel is used for low-dimensional affinities q_ij in t-SNE?
options: Student-t distribution with 1 degree of freedom
items.answer: Student-t distribution with 1 degree of freedom
items.explanation: Heavy-tailed Student-t replaces the Gaussian low-D kernel from SNE to mitigate crowding.

items.question: What is a safe interpretation of two widely separated clusters in a t-SNE plot?
options: Their local neighborhoods differ; global distance between blobs is not reliable
They are exactly twice as far in feature space as nearby clusters
The larger blob always has higher class prevalence
The x-axis units match original feature units
items.answer: Their local neighborhoods differ; global distance between blobs is not reliable
items.explanation: t-SNE preserves local structure; inter-cluster gaps and blob sizes are often misleading.

items.question: Why is PCA sometimes applied before t-SNE?
options: To reduce dimensionality and runtime while keeping most variance for distance geometry
Because t-SNE requires square input matrices
To supply class labels to the KL objective
items.answer: To reduce dimensionality and runtime while keeping most variance for distance geometry
items.explanation: PCA to ~50D is a common speed trick; see the PCA topic for the linear step—t-SNE still handles non-linear layout.
